# Auto-generated blog posts (scheduled)  planned · 2 prototype posts + plumbing shipped 2026-06-10 ✅

**Owner:** [[../functions/cmo]] · **Parent:** CMO mandate "Organic content & SEO"

> **Status:** two real posts render live — `why-people-add-mushrooms-to-their-coffee` (explainer, `how_it_works`) and `iced-brown-sugar-superfood-latte` (recipe, `recipes`), both Amazing Coffee, generated by `scripts/_gen-blog-post.ts` / `_gen-blog-post-2.ts`. Proves the full pipeline: product intelligence + web research → human-voiced article (real citations + real customer phrases) → **Nano Banana Pro** images (hero composites the REAL isolated product pouch; in-body generated) → **WebP compression** → `posts` + `post_products`. **Decisions locked + plumbing built (2026-06-10):** auto-publish, 3 invented author personas, image quick-win (WebP), 4:5 social variant + scheduler pickup, link-in-bio page. Remaining = the scheduled cron + the LLM writer. **Settled open questions at the bottom.**

## Locked decisions + built plumbing (2026-06-10)

- **Auto-publish** — posts go live immediately (`published=true`), no review gate. (Quality held across 2 posts; revisit a spot-check queue only if a bad one slips.)
- **Personas** — 3 invented authors in `src/lib/blog/authors.ts` (registry, not a table yet): **Renee Calhoun** (Recipe Developer), **Priya Anand, RD** (Nutrition Lead — science/explainers), **Marcus Hale** (Wellness Editor — lifestyle). Each has a bio + a generated avatar (`workspaces/{ws}/authors/{slug}.webp`). The writer picks by archetype; `posts.author_slug` stamps it. Rendered as a byline (photo + name + role + date) + an author-bio card + JSON-LD **Person** (E-E-A-T). `author_slug` column added (migration `20260610200000_blog_author_social.sql`).
- **Image optimization (quick-win, done)** — `src/lib/blog/generate-images.ts` runs every Nano Banana output through **sharp → WebP @ ≤1600px** before upload. Measured: a hero went **631KB JPEG → 66KB WebP** (~14× page-weight cut: ~2.3MB → ~167KB). Blog render now `loading="lazy"` + `decoding="async"` on in-body images (`lazyifyHtml`), `fetchPriority="high"` on the LCP hero. **Full AVIF/WebP multi-width pipeline** (reuse [[../libraries/image-transcode]] `transcodeUpload` + `<picture>`/srcset) is the remaining line item — bigger win, applies to imported posts too.
- **Two crops per post** — the hero is rendered **16:9 landscape** (`featured_image_url`, the blog main) and the same scene is re-rendered **4:5 portrait** (1080×1350, `genSocialVariant` → `posts.social_image_url`, what the social poster posts; never shown on the blog). 4:5 is the tallest ratio IG/FB feed allows, so it claims the most vertical real estate. Aspect is forced via Nano Banana Pro's `imageConfig.aspectRatio` (added to `generateNanoBananaProCombine` / `genCompressUpload` — without it the model drifts to square). The organic social scheduler ([[automated-social-scheduler]]) `pickResource` / `pickNewestBlog` prefer `social_image_url` and **fall back to `featured_image_url`** for older posts. `social_image_url` column added in migration `20260610200000_blog_author_social.sql`. *(History: 4:5 → 4:3 on 2026-06-12 → back to 4:5 on 2026-06-16 — Dylan wants social feed posts portrait.)*
- **Link-in-bio page** — `/links` (`store/[workspace]/links/page.tsx`), mobile-first, lists the latest posts + the products they reference. The IG/FB bio link is set to `{domain}/links` **once**; the page always reflects the latest, so every "link in bio" CTA resolves to current content. See § Instagram link-in-bio below.

## Instagram link-in-bio (research → decision)

The social scheduler tells people "link in bio," but **the Instagram Graph API cannot set a profile's bio link/website** — it's read-only for that field ([Elfsight IG Graph API guide](https://elfsight.com/blog/instagram-graph-api-complete-developer-guide-for-2026/)). Instagram *does* support up to **5 native bio links** now, but only editable manually in the app ([multi-link guide](https://addtoqr.com/blog/sharing-multiple-links-in-instagram)). So we can't point the bio at each new post programmatically. **Decision:** a self-hosted **`/links` page we control** — set the bio link to it once (permanent), and it stays current automatically (latest posts + referenced PDPs). This is the standard creator pattern (a self-hosted Linktree) and needs no API access. Future: order `/links` by what was *most recently socially promoted* (join `scheduled_social_posts`), not just newest blog post.

**Goal:** a scheduled engine (target **1 post/day**) that picks a product with [[../lifecycles/product-intelligence|intelligence]], chooses a category + topic from its SEO keywords / ingredients / benefits, researches the web, writes a genuinely useful human-voiced article, generates original branded imagery, and publishes it to the storefront blog ([[../lifecycles/storefront-blog]]).

**The three goals every post must serve one of:**
1. **Rank** on a target topic — pull from [[../tables/product_seo_keywords]] (we have real keyword + Search Console data per product).
2. **Provide value to buyers** — recipes, how-to-use, getting-the-most-out-of guides for people who already own it.
3. **Reinforce value for considerers** — science/explainer/myth-busting that answers the objection a shopper is sitting on.

## Why this is feasible today (prototype findings)

Everything needed already exists:
- **Intelligence** — 7 tables per product ([[../tables/product_ingredients]], [[../tables/product_ingredient_research]] with **real PubMed-style citations**, [[../tables/product_review_analysis]], [[../tables/product_benefit_selections]], [[../tables/product_seo_keywords]]). Amazing Coffee alone has 13 ingredients, 62 research rows, 50 keywords, 1,873 reviews.
- **Image gen** — [[../libraries/gemini]] `generateNanoBananaProCombine` (`gemini-3-pro-image`). Multi-image fusion: pass the **isolated product pouch** + a scene prompt → photoreal hero with the real label intact. Pass `imageUrls: []` → text-to-image for generated in-body shots. Per-workspace key already set.
- **Isolated product shot** — comes from **`product_variants.image_url`** (uploaded to shopcx/Supabase, NOT the Shopify-CDN images in `products.variants` JSON). Verified: `product_variants` pos0/pos1 hold the styled pouch on a light bg — ideal Nano Banana input.
- **Write path** — mirror [[../lifecycles/storefront-blog]] `import-article.ts`: upsert `posts` + replace `post_products`. Images upload to `product-media` bucket at `workspaces/{ws}/posts/{handle}/{slot}.{ext}`.
- **Web research** — `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` (used in the prototype to ground the trend framing + the L-theanine/jitter mechanism).
- **Rendering** — the public blog ([[../lifecycles/storefront-blog]] § Storefront rendering) already renders `posts` with SEO + JSON-LD. No new render work.

## Making them NOT read as AI (the human-likeness problem)

Google doesn't penalize "AI content" — it penalizes **unhelpful, unoriginal, scaled content with no first-hand experience** (E-E-A-T, the "scaled content abuse" policy). The tells that trip it: uniform structure, no original media, no author identity, no first-hand experience, generic phrasing, no proprietary data, robotic publishing velocity. Countermeasures we bake in:

1. **First-hand experience + proprietary data (the #1 lever).** Every post leans on things only we have: our own **customer review phrases** (real outcomes), our **ingredient research + citations**, our recipe testing, our team's POV. "Our customers keep telling us…", "we tested this in our Austin kitchen" — experience signals scrapers can't fake.
2. **Original branded imagery.** The Nano Banana hero **composites our real product photo** — original, on-brand, not stock, not obviously generated. Vary styles across posts. This alone separates us from text-only AI farms. (Prototype hero kept the full "AMAZING COFFEE" label legible — looks genuinely produced.)
3. **Named human authors with real bios + author archive pages.** A small rotating set of personas (recipe developer, nutritionist, founder) each with bio + photo + `/blog/author/{slug}`. JSON-LD `author` as **Person** with `sameAs`. **Never** attribute to "admin" or the org alone. → needs a `posts.author_id` + an `authors` table (see Data model).
4. **Anti-pattern voice rules.** Ban AI tells in the writer prompt: "in today's fast-paced world", "in conclusion", "delve", "unlock", "game-changer", "look no further", "navigating the world of", em-dash overuse, perfectly parallel triads, hedge-everything tone. Enforce **burstiness** (varied sentence length), concrete numbers, sensory detail, real opinions, mild imperfection.
5. **Structural + length variety.** Rotate archetypes (recipe · myth-busting explainer · personal essay · comparison · FAQ deep-dive · seasonal) and templates so 365 posts don't share one skeleton. Vary length 700–1500 words and headline patterns.
6. **Real citations + internal links.** Cite **actual** studies (resolve our citation titles to real DOI/PubMed URLs — never fabricate links). Internal-link to the product + related posts → topical-authority cluster.
7. **Human-in-the-loop gate (recommended at first).** Draft → quick human edit/approve → publish (a "Blog" dashboard review queue), instead of blind auto-publish. A 2-min edit adds real fingerprints and catches hallucinations. Relax to spot-check once trust is established.
8. **Editorial cadence, not robotic.** Stagger publish time-of-day, occasionally skip, vary topic/product. Never dump many at once.
9. **Accuracy guardrails = trust = ranking.** Only cite research we actually have; never overstate; keep the FDA disclaimer + "studies describe ingredients, not the finished product." Hallucinated claims are exactly what gets a site demoted.
10. **Freshness.** `dateModified`, periodic re-touch of older posts, "updated" notes — Google rewards maintained content.
11. **Genuine usefulness.** Recipes people save, guides read to the end → dwell time + return visits, signals AI farms can't manufacture.

## Pipeline (per scheduled run)

```
cron (daily)
  ▼
1. SELECT product   — a product with intelligence_status='published', round-robin / least-recently-posted,
                      weighted toward products with unused SEO keywords.
2. SELECT topic     — choose (category, angle, target keyword) from product_seo_keywords + benefits +
                      ingredients, DEDUPED against existing posts (titles + content_text). Rotate archetype.
3. RESEARCH         — WebSearch/WebFetch for current framing, supporting facts, recipe specifics.
                      Pull our proprietary inputs: ingredient_research (+citations), review phrases.
4. WRITE            — Sonnet/Opus with the anti-AI voice rules + a chosen author persona → content_html
                      (varied structure, real citations, internal links, FDA disclaimer).
5. IMAGES           — Nano Banana Pro: hero = composite(isolated variant pouch + scene prompt);
                      1–2 in-body = generated. Upload to product-media → public URLs → swap into html.
6. PUBLISH          — upsert posts (source='ai_generated', author_id, grouping, is_resource) +
                      post_products. Either publish live or queue for review (open question).
```

## Data model

`posts` is shaped for Shopify imports; for AI posts:
- ✅ **`author_slug` + `social_image_url`** added (migration `20260610200000_blog_author_social.sql`).
- **`shopify_article_id` is `NOT NULL`** — we keep the **`ai:{handle}`** synthetic-key convention (satisfies NOT NULL + the `(workspace_id, shopify_article_id)` unique key + idempotency). Fine as-is; could rename to a generic `external_id` later.
- **Personas** live in a code **registry** (`src/lib/blog/authors.ts`), not an `authors` table — simplest for single-tenant. Promote to an `authors` table + `/blog/author/{slug}` archive pages when multi-tenant or when author pages are wanted (E-E-A-T boost).
- **`source='ai_generated'`** already set. Could add `generation_meta` jsonb (model, keyword, archetype, research URLs, prompt version) for replay/audit — nice-to-have.
- **No review gate** (auto-publish decided). If ever gated: `published=false` + a review state in the Storefront → Blog dashboard.

## Files

Built (2026-06-10):
- ✅ `src/lib/blog/generate-images.ts` — Nano Banana Pro + sharp WebP compression + `genSocialVariant` (4:5).
- ✅ `src/lib/blog/authors.ts` — persona registry + avatars.
- ✅ `src/app/(storefront)/store/[workspace]/links/page.tsx` — link-in-bio page.
- ✅ `posts.author_slug` + `posts.social_image_url` migration; byline/Person rendering; `pickResource` social-image preference.
- ✅ `scripts/_gen-blog-post.ts` / `_gen-blog-post-2.ts` — the working generators (content authored in-script; the LLM writer extracts from these).

The scheduled cron ✅ (2026-06-10):
- ✅ `src/lib/inngest/auto-blog.ts` — daily Inngest cron (`0 13 * * *`, + `auto-blog/tick` event). Per eligible workspace: select → write → images → insert + post_products, auto-published. Per-workspace try/catch (one failure doesn't block others); handle de-dup so it never clobbers an existing post. Registered in `api/inngest/route.ts`.
- ✅ `src/lib/blog/select-topic.ts` — picks a published-intelligence product (round-robin by fewest AI posts), the least-covered archetype (recipes/science/how_it_works/how_to_use), an uncovered SEO keyword, the matching persona, the isolated variant image, and bundles the proprietary intelligence (ingredients, research + citations, review phrases).
- ✅ `src/lib/blog/write-post.ts` — **Opus 4.8 + the Anthropic `web_search_20260209` server tool**: researches live, grounds in the intelligence, writes in-persona under the anti-AI voice rules, emits a delimited block (title/handle/seo/tags/HTML + hero & social image prompts + `{{IMAGE:…}}` in-body placeholders). Resumes through `pause_turn`.
  - **Hero composition picker (`pickComposition`)** — kills the "pouch + mug on a kitchen counter" sameness. The old prompt gave the model one example scene (`e.g. on a sunlit kitchen counter`) and LLMs collapse to it, so every hero looked identical. Now we pick the composition **in code** across 4 independent axes — focal subject (pouch / prepared drink / ingredient flat-lay / lifestyle moment), setting (10 options), camera angle (4), light (4) — decomposed mixed-radix from `seedFrom(product.handle) + existingTitles.length`. Each new post for a product advances the lowest digit (focal subject) first and the combo space fills out as posts accrue; the handle hash offsets the start so products don't open on the same frame. Props stay the writer's job. Social variant mirrors the hero scene in 4:5. Deterministic + reproducible.
- **Eligibility:** any workspace with a published-intelligence product that has an isolated (our-storage) variant image — today that's Superfoods. A per-workspace enable flag + a dashboard "Generate now" button are the follow-ups.

Remaining:
- Dashboard: "Generate now" button + per-workspace enable/cadence config.
- Full AVIF/WebP multi-width image pipeline (reuse [[../libraries/image-transcode]]).

## Open questions — settled / remaining

- ~~Auto-publish vs review queue~~ → **auto-publish** (revisit only if a bad one slips).
- ~~Authors~~ → **3 invented personas** (Renee / Priya / Marcus) in a code registry.
- ~~Link-in-bio~~ → **self-hosted `/links` page** (IG API can't set the bio link).
- ~~Image weight~~ → **WebP quick-win done**; full multi-width pipeline remains.

Remaining for the cron:
1. **Topic priority** — lead with SEO-keyword coverage (goal 1), or rotate the 3 goals across the week?
2. **Which products** — only `intelligence_status='published'` (Amazing Coffee, Amazing Creamer today), or also review-rich products without full intelligence?
3. **Writer model** — Opus (quality, fewer AI-tells) vs Sonnet (cost) at 1/day. Image cost: ~4× Nano Banana Pro/post (hero + 2 body + social).
4. **Volume** — hard 1/day forever, or sprint to cover the keyword set then taper to maintenance + freshness updates?
5. **Citations** — resolve study titles → real PubMed/DOI URLs at write time, or cite by name only (no fabrication risk)?
6. **Recipe schema** — emit `Recipe` JSON-LD on recipe posts (rich results) in addition to `BlogPosting`?
